title: Engineered enzyme strips advanced glycation end-products to reverse age-related protein damage
description: Researchers unveiled an engineered enzyme that removes advanced glycation end-products (AGEs) from proteins, directly targeting a long-standing driver of tissue stiffening and age-related dysfunction. If it translates, AGE-clearing enzymes could address damage that current interventions leave untouched.

title: Restoring calcium-ion homeostasis with a repurposed antidepressant extends lifespan in aging mice
description: Correcting disrupted calcium-ion homeostasis with an existing antidepressant significantly extended median lifespan in aged mice. The result strengthens the case for drug-repurposing approaches that target cellular ion balance as a hallmark of aging.

title: Senolytic vaccine combined with mesenchymal stem cells shows synergistic rejuvenation in mice
description: Pairing a senolytic vaccine that clears senescent cells with mesenchymal stem-cell therapy produced synergistic anti-aging effects in mice beyond either intervention alone. The combination points toward multi-modal rejuvenation protocols rather than single-agent bets.

title: Tau-targeting antisense drug diranersen posts Phase 2 cognitive gains in early Alzheimer's
description: Biogen and Ionis reported Phase 2 CELIA data showing the tau-targeting antisense therapy diranersen (BIIB080) meaningfully reduced tau and slowed cognitive decline in early Alzheimer's. Brain aging remains one of the highest-value fronts in the longevity field.

title: 8-hour time-restricted eating raises male-mouse median lifespan by 12%
description: A controlled study found that restricting feeding to an 8-hour daily window increased median lifespan in male mice by roughly 12%. The finding reinforces time-restricted eating as one of the most accessible, evidence-backed longevity protocols.

title: Engineered enzyme reverses age-related protein damage
description: Scientists developed an enzyme that removes advanced glycation end-products from proteins to combat age-related molecular damage.

title: Activating GPR40 receptor fights thymic involution in mice
description: Targeting the GPR40 receptor delayed thymus aging and helped preserve immune function in aging mice.

title: Forever Healthy launches Evipedia AI integration
description: Forever Healthy introduced tools to open its longevity evidence database to AI research pipelines and agents.

title: Peripheral inflammation may drive Parkinson's disease
description: A study indicates peripheral tissue inflammation can spread to the brain via extracellular vesicles, implicating it in Parkinson's.

title: Gabriel Cian on building the 2060 longevity ecosystem
description: An interview on how the longevity ecosystem is evolving, with lessons from the past year of investment and policy.

title: Combining senolytics and stem cells shows promise in mice
description: A senolytic vaccine combined with mesenchymal stem cells produced synergistic anti-aging effects in mouse studies.

title: An experimental proposal for blocking ambient radiation
description: A perspective proposes underground laboratory facilities to test whether removing surface radiation slows aging.

title: Optimizing health guidelines toward optimal outcomes
description: An analysis argues UK exercise and protein-intake guidelines should be optimized for better long-term health outcomes.

title: Rescuing calcium ion homeostasis extends mouse lifespan
description: Correcting disrupted calcium homeostasis with an antidepressant significantly extended lifespan in aging mice.

title: How muscle loss and bone loss are related in aging
description: New work clarifies the shared mechanisms linking sarcopenia and osteoporosis as the body ages.

title: Intermittent fasting increases lifespan in male mice
description: Time-restricted feeding to an 8-hour window increased median lifespan in male mice by about 12 percent.

title: Rejuvenation Roundup: June 2026
description: A monthly summary of advances across genomic approaches and other areas of aging research.

title: Study maps existing drugs to the hallmarks of aging
description: A network-analysis method predicts whether existing medications could extend human lifespan by acting on aging hallmarks.

title: How a primate-specific RNA strand worsens senescence
description: Researchers identified a primate-specific non-coding RNA linked to cellular senescence and aging.

title: Cell type-specific aging predicts disease onset
description: Cell-type-specific aging trajectories were shown to predict diseases including Alzheimer's and lung cancer.

title: Diranersen Phase 2 CELIA data show tau reduction and cognitive benefit
description: Biogen presented Phase 2 CELIA results showing the tau-targeting antisense therapy diranersen (BIIB080) reduced tau and delivered cognitive improvements in early Alzheimer's.

title: FDA approves gedatolisib, first pan-PI3K/mTOR inhibitor for breast cancer
description: The FDA approved Celcuity's gedatolisib (Revtorpyk); the pan-PI3K and mTOR inhibitor showed a 76% reduction in disease-progression risk in the VIKTORIA-1 trial, notable given mTOR's central role in aging.

title: Bempikibart meets primary endpoint for hair regrowth in alopecia areata
description: Q32 Bio's SIGNAL-AA Phase 2a trial of the anti-IL-7 receptor antibody bempikibart achieved meaningful hair regrowth in severe alopecia areata.

title: Denecimig long-term safety confirmed in hemophilia A extension study
description: Novo Nordisk's Phase 3 FRONTIER4 extension confirmed sustained efficacy and favorable safety for the bispecific antibody denecimig.

title: Vitamin B12 deficiency can mimic normal aging
description: A shortage of B12, though needed in tiny amounts, can produce significant energy and health impacts that resemble aging.

title: Osteopenia is silently weakening bones in millions
description: The overlooked condition of reduced bone density is often discovered only after fractures occur.

title: Butterfly that barely ages could help unlock longevity secrets
description: Heliconius butterflies show extended lifespans with minimal decline, offering clues to the biology of slow aging.

title: Long-lived families reveal a rare genetic clue to healthy aging
description: Rare genetic variants in long-lived families may suppress inflammation and extend healthy lifespan.

title: Yale study finds nearly half of older adults improved with age
description: Research contradicts aging stereotypes, finding physical and mental improvements in many adults over 65.

title: Biotech VC funding tracker surpasses $18.0B raised year-to-date in 2026
description: A continuously updated tracker of 2026 biotech venture rounds passed $18.0B raised year-to-date, reflecting sustained capital inflows into the sector including longevity-adjacent startups.
